calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Assignments By The Heirs
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hilary Hedemark for (administrator) Frances Bruton. Daniel Conrad for the 16 Community Property Heirs.

The Court stated that there are issues raised by the examiner regarding the courts interest in measuring efforts by heir hunting companies earning fees. Ms. Hedemark reported that the heirs were predeceased spouses, nieces and nephews and that they would have had to hire a private investigator whose rates would have been higher. Mr. Conrad reported that he filed a declaration, that he has worked with Brandenburger & Davis for many years, that they are an ethical company, that a 15% assignment is very low, that Mr. Conrad himself is only earning 1/2 of his hourly rate, that all 16 people were entitled to an inheritance, that they all received their shares, that the case itself was complicated, that there was a dispute with a tenant that had to be resolved, that all of the clients are happy that they were located and that there was no duress or fraud. The Court stated that it has concerns in general, that it is the courts role to measure efforts and have not been given anything to do that and that the nature of the relationships should have been part of the petition process. Mr. Conrad stated that he understands the courts concerns, cited 11604 & 11604.5, and that another 15% assignment was filed and approved and that he believes this issue arose as he signed the receipts with power of attorney. Mr. Conrad requested that if the court is going to lower the rates to Brandenburger & Davis that it issue an Order to Appear to give them a chance to submit documentation to respond to the court's concerns. Mr. Conrad asked if the court would like the assignments filed in the future and the court responded that it would. The status on filing of assignments by the heirs was taken under submission.

On September 3, 2015 an order for final discharge was signed and this matter is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Assignments By The Heirs
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Continued To Sep-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Assignments By The Heirs
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Continued To Aug-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hilary Hedemark for (Administrator) Frances Bruton.

The court stated that there is a question regarding the review on the documentation of time. Ms. Hedemark reported that she has talked to Daniel Conrad who represents 15 of the 40 heirs, that $10,000.00 represents his fees, that 15% of the assignment is on the low end, that they feel it is reasonable for the two years spent trying to find the heirs and that she spoke to an investigator who confirmed that it would have cost considerably more if she had hired someone to find the heirs. The court requested that Ms. Hedemark obtain and file a declaration from Mr. Conrad regarding his time and fees and continued the status of filing assignments by the heirs to August 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hilary Hedemark for Frances Bruton (Petitioner).

Ms. Hedemark stated that the receipt for the blocked account has been submitted to Chase Bank, that she was advised on Friday that it has been submitted to Chase Bank's legal department and that she anticipates having it filed by this Friday. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account was continued to August 28, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hilary Hedemark for and with Frances Bruton.

Ms. Hedemark stated that there is no relative named Imogene and that she double checked with Mr. Corum's Attorney. Ms. Hedemark also stated that the bank accounts have a balance of $542,000.00 and $3,000.00, and that they intend to sell the real property. The Court informed Ms. Hedemark that it would like a drop off ex parte petition revoking prior letters which is to include a declaration indicating that there is no such person named Imogene. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed was taken under submission (subject to filing of the ex parte petition). The issue of bond will be addressed by the Court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
